summary: Generate a full comic-style PDF using ReportLab (Platypus), with true panel-based comic layout (not just dialogue), a consistent color scheme/theme, and credits/authoring details.
architectural_notes: Use ReportLab Platypus (SimpleDocTemplate, Paragraph, Table/TableStyle) to build comic-style panel layouts and credits pages.
Extend ReportLab Platypus layout to support colored comic panel styling (background fills, borders, theme palette) so the PDF reads as a real comic rather than a dialogue transcript.

existing_features.title: Add a deduplication step for comic dialogue/panel text so that repeated lines are removed from the generated comic content before rendering/exporting.
Expose a clear, user-facing indication that repetition removal is active (e.g., a toggle or label) and ensure all user-facing text is in English.
Apply a coherent, comic-inspired visual theme across the frontend (colors, typography, spacing, and panel styling) and use it consistently throughout the app.

Replace the current string-only story/credits data model with a structured comic panel model that supports distinct comic parts (e.g., panel title/scene label, narration/caption boxes, speech bubbles, thought bubbles, and sound-effect text), so the UI can render an actual comic instead of a transcript.

Update the comic reading experience to visually look like a real comic: render panel frames with consistent comic styling, and within each panel render distinct comic parts with clear visual differentiation (caption boxes, speech bubbles, SFX typography) and a coherent, intentional color scheme across panels.

Ensure the existing repetition-removal behavior remains compatible with the new structured panel model by deduplicating repeated panel text content in a predictable way (e.g., deduplicate repeated dialogue/caption/SFX lines across panels while preserving the first occurrence).

new_feature_requests.summary: Ensure generated comic panels/dialogue do not contain repetition (deduplicate or avoid repeated lines).
Render an actual comic in the PDF with proper comic panels/parts and a coherent color scheme (not just pure dialogue/transcript).
Ensure the generated PDF remains screen-reader friendly / accessible for audio reading.
Include author credit/name in the PDF (Andy Romero Escobar J.).

projectName: Prevent repeated dialogue/panels in generated comic content
